Section - 1 Introduction:

What is a Village Ecological Register?

The Village Ecological Register is a document prepared by community members, systematically

capturing information about a village's traditional ecological knowledge - systems and practices and

local biodiversity. It serves as a repository for accumulating knowledge, insights, and data, providing a

This participatory approach will encourage the direct involvement of the community in observing and

recording environmental changes. This will also serve as a springboard for stimulating ecological

orientation and sensitivity amidst the community members. It will also provide an opportunity for

initiating comprehensive discussions within the community concerning ecological indicators.

Why is there a need for preparing a Village Ecological Register?

The primary aim of instituting a village ecological register is to proactively involve communities in

recording the oral traditional ecological knowledge systems and practices along with it’s rich
